page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ae.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 2 dedicated physical cores of an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9T34 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The instance features 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include any local storage or GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks show a significant divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng, is in the top tier (top 10%) at 3111.39. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance is weak, scoring 3112.08, which places it in the bottom 25%. Large-block memory bandwidth is average, with read speeds at 47.71 GB/sec and write speeds at 27.73 GB/sec, while small-block cached read bandwidth is weak at 153.06 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 (with VNNI and BF16) and AES/VAES cryptographic acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g8ae.large provides dedicated CPU allocation, ensuring that compute resources are not shared with other tenants. The primary tradeoff is the limited multi-threaded scaling, as the dual-core configuration restricts performance in highly concurrent environments. It represents a cost-efficient option for workloads that require high-frequency single-core execution without the need for massive parallel processing.

This instance is appropriate for lightweight general-purpose workloads, single-threaded application hosting, development environments, and basic utility scripting. It is less optimal for database operations, concurrent web serving, or storage-heavy applications due to its weak multi-core benchmark results and lack of local storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ae.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC 9T34 cores clocked at 3.4 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU resources. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU execution, contrasted by weak multi-core and database performance. The instance supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AES cryptography. It represents a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring dedicated, high-frequency single-threaded compute, such as lightweight application hosting, development environments, and basic utility scripts, while being less suited for highly concurrent or storage-intensive tasks.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core compute instance delivering top-tier single-threaded performance and balanced memory for lightweight general-purpose workloads.

Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM and 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance in benchmarks
